summary: Errors are thrown in output window when accessing a global node's tree/world from another global node during editing

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4, 4.3, 4.2.2, 4.1.4, 4.0.4, 4.0-alpha1

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7260) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9700K CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Trying to access a global Node's world or tree in another global Node gives an error as if the function is being called, example:

```gdscript
extends Node

## Inside Global Script
## PlayerChar is another Global Script

PlayerChar.get_world_3d(). # <- typing this period will trigger the error in the output window

# ERROR scene/3d/node_3d.cpp:892 - Condition "!is_inside_world()" is true. Returning: Ref<World3D>()

```

In my personal project these errors do not trigger during runtime because PlayerChar is properly placed in a tree
But during editing these errors are thrown
I have a minimal example project to help give more context to this strange issue

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open MRP 
2. Open `player_settings.gd`
3. After any of the function calls in `foo()`, add a period, accessing the object those function calls are returning (comments are added for extra context in the script)
4. Error should appear in output window

summary: Fix missing alpha input for visual shaders

description.text:
Easy 1 line fix https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/103885

This allows us to use the alpha channel as an accumulator across lighting passes for any given pixel when overriding the lighting function in the visual shader editor. Without this, the last light to run for a pixel can only overwrite the alpha completely.

As a demonstration this is the "Shadow to Opacity" recreated with this change [(for reference)](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/4.0/servers/rendering/renderer_rd/shaders/scene_forward_lights_inc.glsl#L235).

![godot windows editor dev x86_64_toCxXWojmA](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3a93e62-794b-42a0-a68e-6ddd28885e70)

summary: Visual shader light graph lacks access to 'alpha' accumulator

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4

### System information

Windows 10, Nvidia, not super relevant to this issue

### Issue description

When composing Visual Shaders, and overriding the "Light" function, the "Input" node is missing Alpha from it's list, which is unlike the Diffuse and Specular in the lighting function.

This means you cannot properly accumulate alpha values across light calculations. This is something you CAN do in hand-coded shaders. So effects similar to "Shadow to Opacity" not possible to reproduce in the visual shader editor. The last light to touch the alpha output for a given pixel has no context about the other passes, it'll be overwriting it completely very time.

The fix is actually simple and I'm throwing a PR up for it next.

### Steps to reproduce

* Just take a look at the input dropdown in the "Light" function of a visual shader graph, there's no way to access Alpha across light passes.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

* Just take a look at the input dropdown in the "Light" function of a visual shader graph, there's no way to access Alpha across light passes.

summary: Build Profile "Detect from Project" Causes Error: Mainloop type doesn't exist: SceneTree

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.4.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 7 7735HS with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

Using "Detect from project" in the engine compilation configuration editor causes the build to crash on launch. I detected from project, saved the `.build` file, then compiled Windows release templates using it. Exporting the MRP with that build makes it crash on launch with this error: "Mainloop type doesn't exist: Scenetree"

![Clipboard - March 9, 2025](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd739e37-cd2e-4ba3-9811-59f8b1f8f9a9)

**This issue doesn't happen when I reset to the default settings then tick off the same nodes/resources manually**. Looking at the autogenerated `.build` file, we can clearly see the issue: it includes "MainLoop" in the list of disabled classes which Godot apparently needs to run. Removing it from the file fixes things... Sort of. 

This should probably be its own separate issue, but it's worth noting in this same MRP, the autodetect feature fails to include other things the game needs to run. The game crashes on launch due to not having StyleBoxFlat: `ERROR: Class 'StyleBoxFlat' or its base class cannot be instantiated.` After removing StyleBox from the list of disabled classes, it now causes an error that it can't find KinematicCollision2D, which should definitely be included when 2D physics bodies exist in the project.

This thing is too overzealous in what it removes. I'm tired of fighting the build editor for one day 😪.

[build_files.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19154423/build_files.zip)


### Steps to reproduce

Open up MRP, go to Project>Tools>Engine Compilation Configuration Editor

Click on "Detect from project", then save as a new `.build` file

Try compiling a release template and use it to export the MRP. The build created will fail to launch with the error mentioned above.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[godot-size-test_2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19154431/godot-size-test_2.zip)

summary: `get_tree()` is called on non-tool autoloads in-editor

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Identified in v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]
- Reproduced in 4.3.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA T500 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6076) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-1135G7 @ 2.40GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Assume I have an autoload named `Autoload`. If I write `Autoload.get_tree().` in another script, I will immediately see `ERROR: scene/main/node.h:485 - Parameter "data.tree" is null.` output to console, indicating that something, for some reason, is trying to call `get_tree()` while I'm writing the line. This behavior occurs on a fresh test project with no tool scripts or plugins.

This is also true for writing `get_world_3d().` - `ERROR: scene/3d/node_3d.cpp:892 - Condition "!is_inside_world()" is true. Returning: Ref<World3D>()`

This is also true for writing `get_path().` - `ERROR: Cannot get path of node as it is not in a scene tree.`

I have also tested a few other functions, such as `get_tree_string()` and `get_global_transform_interpolated()`, but without any results.

The errors only appear when referencing the autoload from another script; writing `get_tree().` in the autoload itself produces no output.

Note that the errors only appear when inputting the `.` after the function call.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a script and assign it as an autoload in Project Settings (named `Autoload` in this example).
- Create any other script.
- In the second script, begin writing a line that includes `Autoload.get_tree().`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: UV scrolling + Normal map not possible at 2D canvas_item fragment shader

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducile in : v4.1.1.stable.official [bd6af8e0e]

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot v4.1.1.stable.official [bd6af8e0e]

### Issue description

When assigning a value to NORMAL_MAP, immediatelly all of the default lighting calculations are lost.
Even if it is just a UV shift.

I am trying to make your everyday tiled-scrolling-shader.
``` 
vec4 tex = texture(TEXTURE, uvt); // uvt is UV shifted by some TIME-based speed
```

But with a minor twist that I want to combine it with normal maps. One would think all you have to do is shifting the normals along with the color.
```
vec4 norm = texture(NORMAL_MAP_TEX, uvt); // "hint_normal" yada yada...
```

But that doesn't work. As soon as you write anything at the NORMAL_MAP variable, all of the light calculations are lost, the default behavior is gone.
I've tried looking for some shader that manually replicates the default one within the Shader Editor, but I could not find it either. So I can't manually calculate the light the exact same way.

This isssue has been haunting me for years now. Am I missing something? Is this the intended behavior? If I just shift the normal maps, I need to recalculate all of the lighting from scratch?

- Possibly related to: #56528

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a canvas_item 2D fragment shader
2. Create a Sprite, TextureRect or Line2D
3. Assign a CanvasTexture to it, and fill it with Diffuse and Normal Textures
4. Set some evident Shininess for the Specular [0.3 ~ 0.6].
5. Create new ShaderMaterial and Shader.
6. Try to scroll the UVs of COLOR and NORMAL_MAP at same time while preserving the default light behavior (code below).

```
shader_type canvas_item;

uniform float speed : hint_range(0.0, 3.0, 0.05) = 0.75;
uniform sampler2D NORMAL_MAP_TEX : hint_normal, filter_linear, repeat_disable;

void fragment() {
	vec2 uvt = vec2(fract(UV.x - TIME * speed), UV.y);
	vec4 tex = texture(TEXTURE, uvt);
	vec4 norm = texture(NORMAL_MAP_TEX, uvt);
	COLOR.rgba = tex;
	NORMAL_MAP = norm.rgb;
	NORMAL_MAP_DEPTH = norm.b;
}
```

summary: Some SVG imports with embedded PNG are crashing Godot when previously they weren't (ThorVG 0.14.2+ regression)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot Engine v4.4.stable.official.4c311cbee - https://godotengine.org

### System information

Windows 10, CPU: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor 3.80 GHz, GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080

### Issue description

TL;DR: 
6 out of 466 of my svgs have started crashing Godot when it tries to import them when everything was working fine previously.

I opened my project today and certain svgs started crashing godot immediately, which is really weird because all of this worked previously with no issues what-so-ever for the last few months. Originally I was on Gotdot 4.3 but ended up converting to 4.4 in hopes of it resolving my issue to no avail. I will say however that I am pretty sure all of these resources were originally imported in a version below 4.3 probably 4.1 or 4.2 and then I upgraded to 4.3 at a later date and now 4.4. To also note these svgs have not been edited in around ~6 months time so there should not be anything new with them. I did do a project restructure this January which is when I upgraded to 4.3 though most of that had to do with restructuring the folders of the project and not the contents but even that had been working fine for the past month.

I have run into import issues in the past with Godot so I went through and started clearing godot caches forcing a full reimport of all my assets. Unfortunately this did not fix the issue. The thing that is weird is that 98% of the svgs in my project are not causing crashes like this and they are all created using the same software and exported using the same settings. I had to go through and take all my svg folders and add a .gdignore to them all to make sure the engine could startup and then I painstakingly removed a handful of the .gdignore files at a time until I found the consistently offending svgs.

Things I noticed while debugging this issue:
 - Doing a bulk import of every svg resources caused more of the svgs to fail
	- Though they would generally fail on the same file each time
 - Most svgs that failed in the bulk import were able to be imported one at a time without issue
 - I now have 6 svg assets that immediately crash godot even when imported one at a time

I found some issues others posted about as well that I tried to work through:
 - https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/94112
 - https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/91781

The most notable of these is 91781 which talked about having embedded pngs caused the crash but in my reproduction project I have a working svg with an embedded png(Specular_1.svg) so that doesn't seem to be the issue.

As far as 94112 I have tried switching the project to use compatibility mode but it still crashes.

I have not had the time to try to compile godot myself to get the debug symbols for an actual trace versus the below set of nondescript errors, but wanted to see if anyone had run into this issue or if there is a known solution.

```

summary: Allow opening a project in verbose mode from the Project Manager

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11956.

**NOTE:** The description below is outdated. In its current state, this PR simply adds another item to the dropdown menu next to the Project Manager's "Edit" button for "Edit in verbose mode". The ability to hold the <kbd>Alt</kbd> key in order to activate verbose mode has been removed.

---

This PR adds the ability to open projects in verbose mode from the Project Manager. By holding down the Alt/Option key, the "Edit" and "Run" buttons become "Edit (Verbose)" and "Run (Verbose)". Clicking them while holding Alt/Option passes the `--verbose` flag to the Godot process that the Project Manager runs.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c954b71b-b293-4845-a551-9f7b8748e56f

## Issues
- As can be seen in the video above, adding "(Verbose)" to the button labels causes the right-hand panel to change its width. This doesn't look very good to me, so I'd like to see if there's a way to keep the width consistent.
- If the user holds Alt/Option, then selects "Edit in recovery mode", the Project Manager loses track of the Alt/Option key's state. When they click "Cancel" from the recovery mode confirmation window, the Project Manager still thinks that Alt/Option is being held down, and doesn't return to regular "Edit" and "Run" buttons until the user presses and lets go of Alt/Option again.

summary: Inspector display eventually loses correlation with .tscn data in an Inherited scene from blender animated rig .glb

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4 (and previows versions, this isn't something new)

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

This is a (considerably horrible imo) issue anyone working with mesh or animation exports later inherited into a scene shouldn't take long to encounter.

After a while of having a .tscn created as inherited scene from an exported file (in my case a 3D rig made and exported from blender as .glb) it's quite guaranteed that any data displayed in inspector will stop correlating to the data in the actual inherited .tscn, with data tending to accumulate in the .tscn from changes done in inspector.

Here's a typed dictionary example I just noticed while trying it on godot 4.4:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e4c626ee-dc6d-4584-a77b-64b4704b6f05)
As you can see, there are far more accumulated entries on the .tscn file than on the editor, and the data doesn't even correlate too well. It's like the inherited scene can no longer be modified properly.

*Edit:* The persistent data for this dictionary (partially shown in image) is now for some reason on the top level node where the inherited rig scene is placed (the character node above the rig) and not in the .tscn for the inherited rig itself, this is likely very related to this issue, and makes a lot of sense as to why the editor dictionary is not displaying what's actually being persistently stored.

I mentioned [this issue on the godot forums](https://forum.godotengine.org/t/new-typed-dictionaries-and-evaluator-tab/104716) when I noticed my typed dictionary iterated values weren't correlating to what was displayed in the inspector. I later did a test on a clean project and typed dictionary was working fine, that's when I remembered people often encounter this issue when working with inherited scenes from a model file.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Import a 3D rig to godot as .glb and make a derived .tscn from it.
2. Attach a script to the root node and export + define/initialize some variables for it (a dictionary or array may be an easier types for visualizing the problem).
3. At some point <yet undetermined> the data displayed in inspector for the derived .tscn loses correlation with the data displayed in inspector @ export's. It's possible an extra sequence of steps could easily trigger this disconnection but it is as of yet unknown. This is why I say anyone doing this sort of workflow will eventually encounter the issue, because it just starts happening after some time working on a project.

- I currently don't know how the file has to be made, or if it's just blender files, or what kind of data they need to contain, it could potentially be any export with any data from any program one makes a derived scene out of.
- I do know other people have had this issue with other kinds of exported files they then created inherited scenes from (not necessarily rigs).
- There may also be other mentions of the same issue on this issue tracker, arrived to from different reproduction steps, but I believe they have most likely been neglected and/or erroneously closed.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I understand this is hard to debug with the information I'm providing, and I currently cannot provide an MRP for the same reason, but will try to provide one and refine this issue report (if still open) in the near future as I continue my work.

If the issue is closed for no MRP, I can at least note the ideas already commented on this issue for a better rewrite of it.

It's best to actually have mentions to this very annoying issue than no one actually reporting it just because it's hard to reproduce.

In Godot, the Popup control class is the subclass of the Window class. So sometimes the Popup's behavior is more like a window, not the true Popup.</del>

### Issue description

In the Forward+ and Mobile renderers, when rendering the engine interface and in-game UI elements (for example, health bars), sprites, static thin dark stripes are observed along the edges of these elements. Artifacts do not appear on all elements and are localized in certain areas of the screen, i.e. they appear in the same screen coordinates. The artifacts also depend on the zoom. I scaled the viewport 2D.

Create a project with a Forward+ or Mobile renderer. Artifacts are already visible if you look closely.

summary: Add auto translate mode for items in `PopupMenu` and `OptionButton`

description.text:
Make i18n for popup menus easier if only some items need auto translation. Similar to #97519 and #97353.

For example, the `Editor / Layout` submenu has to translate shortcut names (Save Layout, Load Layout, etc) but should not translate custom layout names. Currently, all items in this submenu are not translated as a result of #99158.

Also made some common popup menus in the editor to better utilize (per-item) auto translation mode.

summary: Fix stuck editor cameras and fix 3D error spam for non-finite transforms

description.text:
Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/80755 in a better way, fixes #96824, supersedes #102966.

Before https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/81076, in Godot 4.1 and earlier, Godot allowed INF/NAN in Range. This meant that it was possible to use INF/NAN for node transforms. The problem is that the editor code doesn't have any checks to handle this case. In both 2D and 3D, focusing a node at a non-finite position would break the editor camera. Additionally, in 3D, selecting a node with a non-finite transform would cause lots of errors to print to the console.

After https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/81076, in Godot 4.2 to 4.4, Godot does not allow INF/NAN in Range. This meant that the user could not type INF/NAN to set a node to a non-finite transform. However, this broke the ability to use INF/NAN values elsewhere, and broke the display of existing INF/NAN values.

More importantly, https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/81076 did not actually fix the underlying bug, so https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/80755 is actually not fixed yet. If you set a node to have a non-finite transform through means other than the editor inspector, such as editing a `.tscn` file or using a `@tool` script, then the same bug is still present in Godot 4.4-stable: you can break the editor camera and get error spam.

In PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100414 I proposed to allow Range to use INF/NAN again, but this would make the underlying bug https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/80755 easier to trigger, so I am making this PR which should be merged first.

This PR adds checks to the 2D and 3D editor code to skip non-finite transforms when focusing the editor camera, calculating bounding boxes, and positioning gizmos. This fixes the camera breaking and fixes the error spam in 3D.

summary: Add Embed Game Window shortcut hotkeys support for `suspend/pause` and `next frame` buttons

description.text:
Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/11948

This add shortcuts for the `suspend/pause` and `next frame` buttons inside the embed window.

I've set the following default hotkeys
- F9 to toggle suspend/pause
- F10 to advance to next frame

They are the same hotkeys as `Breakpoint` and `Step into` hotkeys. But they don't conflict with each other due to different context focus.

The F9 hotkey is using the same convention as the breakpoint hotkey uses for macos.
So the hotkey is: `KeyModifierMask::META | KeyModifierMask::SHIFT | Key::B`

I've **intentionally** let the next frame button work with is_echo() so you can hold the button to skip several frames.

This adds a very systematic way to add new shortcuts for the embed window buttons if any more buttons would like to have hotkeys. More info on the steps are inside the issue.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/73452966-b5b2-4ee6-886d-6e386d4829eb

summary: Fix CurveEdit not notifying about all changes

description.text:
Closes #103831

Unlike `Curve::set_point_value()`, `Curve::set_point_offset()` doesn't mark curve as dirty which causes problems with `CurveEdit::set_point_position()` that calls them out of order and causes `CurveTexture` resource to not use the latest data of its `Curve`

This could be solved by adding `mark_dirty()` to `Curve::set_point_offset` but that would double the amount of calls to heavy `CurveTexture::_update`. So instead we're reversing the order of calls in `CurveEdit::set_point_position`. I made sure `set_point_value` is using the correct index (it's known in advance because drag has already happened)

summary: No method for setting an Texture2DRD with an rd_format_srgb resulting in incorrect display in either 3d or 2d

description.text:
### Tested versions

godot 4.3

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable (77dcf97d8) - Windows 10.0.19045 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.3161) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8750H CPU @ 2.20GHz (12 Threads)

### Issue description

As the title says I don't think that the feature exists (this might not be the correct place to raise this - let me know if not), but I am raising it as a bug since it results in Texture2DRD resources displaying either too pale in 3D or too dark in 2D neither of which is ideal.

Overview of the issue: 

Create a Texture2DRD resource, assign it a nice color texture, view it in 3D, it looks OK, view it in 2D, it looks too dark. Think, Ok, so it should be converted to SRGB, do so, look good in 2D, view in 3D, look too light.

Workarounds:
- There are of course things we could do here, if the texture is OK for 2D (i.e. it is in SRGB) you can set your 3D shader to convert to linear. 
- You can duplicate all textures and convert the dups.

None of these are ideal as each requires additional work for each created resource. 
If this were a few versions ago I'd assume it was a limitation and ignore it, or use one of the above workarounds. However I read that this was solved with bindless textures in vulcan (not sure what that means), and I have seen that it works once I have exported my Texture2DRD as a png, see below. 

I have looked at how this is handled in the engine and from what I can tell there is a `rd_format_srgb` member on the `RD::TextureStorage::texture` object which when set will generate a shared texture with a view that converts the texture to SRGB for 2D.
I think this is how it functions at least, please correct me if I am off.


This is what it looks like in game, here in 3D I have my Texture2DRD (data in linear space) on the left on the cube, and a PNG the right (the png was created by converting the Texture2DRD to SRBG and then exporting as an image, since PNG is assumed to be SRGB).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a295e75e-64c6-4128-807f-8bd88f0c7633)

And here is the 2D view of these two image side by side, again RD on the left and PNG on the right.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9b1cdf89-f4c0-405f-affd-08a9f3911016)




### Steps to reproduce

Create a Texture2DRD resource, assign it a nice color texture, view it in 3D, it looks OK, view it in 2D, it looks too dark. Think, Ok, so it should be converted to SRGB, do so, look good in 2D, view in 3D, look too light.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

NA

summary: The third-party license window does not accomplish its intentions, and should be an addon instead of in core.

description.text:
### Tested versions

N/A

### System information

N/A

### Issue description

#79599 introduces the license notice dialog in core to "Providing zero (or minimal) effort way for license compliance."

But this purpose can be easily solved by auto-generating a license file in the export folder. On platforms like Web/Mobile where the file cannot be bundled with the game, the license dialog is also not accessible without a keyboard.
It requires the developer to manually add a button to show the dialog, rendering this PR awkward as the developers need to first know that the license is a thing before they can add the button to show the dialog.
If a developer already knows the license stuff and is willing to show it in their game, they'll either make their own UI that fits their game, or download the license dialog as an addon.
The dialog itself is fairly simple and is not eligible enough to be in core. And the default dialog integration seems taking away the control from the developers and making decisions for them without asking their opinions. Many developers won't even notice that there's a hidden license dialog in their game without their permission.

If the team supports the solution of the license dialog, it should be an addon with editor support. When the project is loaded for the first time or an option is selected in the tools submenu, a prompt should pop up to tell the developer about the license thing, advises them to add a button in their game to show the licenses, asks whether to add the license dialog addon and sets the shortcut up.

My opinion is that #79599 should be reverted, and this license compliance dialog integration thing should be discussed thoroughly and implemented cautiously.

summary: TileMapLayer ignores filtering/mipmaps on small transparent textures, causing flicker when zoomed out

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:
- 4.4-stable
- 4.3-stable
- 4.2.2-stable (using a TileMap instead of a TileMapLayer)
- 4.1.4-stable (using a TileMap instead of a TileMapLayer)
- 4.0.4-stable (using a TileMap instead of a TileMapLayer)

Not reproducible in:
- 3.6-stable (made a second project for it)

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable.mono - Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS 22.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-12900KS (24 threads)

### Issue description

When using a TileMapLayer to display textures showing stars on a transparent background, the textures flicker (“glitter”) at sub-pixel sizes during zoom out.

If the same texture is used as a simple Sprite node, filtering and mipmaps work correctly and no flicker occurs. This suggests a bug in the TileMapLayer rendering path.

- Expected behavior: The engine’s filtering and mipmap settings should smooth or reduce flicker, as they do when using a normal Sprite node.
- Actual behavior: The stars in the TileMap still appear to jump around at sub-pixel scale, ignoring filtering/mipmaps.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e335e53c-6486-44c3-8b05-a7ac13e36027)

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce: 
- Import an image of white/grey stars on transparent background, with mipmaps autogeneration enabled.
- Create a TileMapLayer in Godot 4+ and assign a TileSet with the texture previously created by the import.
- Set Default Texture Filter as Linear Mipmap in Rendering > Textures project settings.
- Zoom out below ~0.2 or so.
- Observe that the star tiles flicker at sub-pixel sizes, unlike the same texture when used as a Sprite.

To use the MRP, simply open it in Godot 4.4 and launch the scene. Using the mouse wheel to zoom out, the flickering appears.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Joined are two minimal projects:
- One for Godot 4.4 that shows the bug

summary: `@export_tool_button` uses separate versions of variables with persistent state between runs

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4stable

### System information

MacOS Sequoia 15.3.1

### Issue description

When decorating a function with @export_tool_button, any variables edited in that decorated function don't revert changes when stopping and rerunning a project. The variables' values also _never_ line up with those read inside functions run any other way once there's a change to their value (i.e., running a function with @export_tool_button with give different values for variables it accesses than the same function triggered by a keypress). This persistent state can be reset by quitting and reopening the project or by detaching and reattaching the problem script from a node. At minimum this effects arrays, classes, and floats.

### Steps to reproduce

To reproduce:
 - Download or copy the project/code below into a new project
 - Run the project
 - Press the inspector button a few times (the console will show the int variable increasing)
 - Pressing space shows that `_input()` is reading a different value for `foo` than the decorated `bar()` is
 - Stop running the project
 - Run the project again
 - Press the inspector button again to show that the changes made in the last run of the project are still there
 - And pressing space again shows those changes are only within the decorated version of `bar()`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

MRP Project: [exporttoolissue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19145315/exporttoolissue.zip)
Minimum reproduction code:
```
@tool
extends Object

@export_tool_button("Button") var action = bar
var foo = 1

func bar() -> void:
	foo += 1
	print(foo)

func _input(_event: InputEvent):
	if Input.is_key_pressed(KEY_SPACE):

summary: Fix macOS mouse tracking in tooltip popups

description.text:
Fixes #100682.

On macOS, `EditorHelpBitTooltip` would never receive `NOTIFICATION_WM_MOUSE_ENTER` or `NOTIFICATION_WM_MOUSE_EXIT`, causing the faulty behavior described in the linked issue.

This is due to an underlying problem with the popups' instances of `GodotContentView` (the `NSView` subclass that hosts their window content)—these instances would never receive their `mouseEntered` or `mouseExited` messages.

~~I was unable to determine exactly why these messages were not being sent—here were a few items I checked:~~
- ~~The view correctly sets its `frame` and tracking area~~
- ~~The view always returns `YES` from `acceptsFirstResponder`~~
- ~~There don't seem to be any other overlapping `NSView`s on top that could intercept the events~~

It seems a popup's window never becomes the key window, so it would not receive events due to the `NSTrackingActiveInKeyWindow` option (per Apple docs: "The owner receives messages when the view is in the key window").

This PR replaces `NSTrackingActiveInKeyWindow` with `NSTrackingActiveWhenFirstResponder` in the tracking area options, which allows the popups to receive these events due to their first responder status, despite not being in the key window.

I'm unsure if this change would cause adverse side effects—
This is roughly the edge of my Godot/AppKit knowledge so any further insight is appreciated!

Edit: The PR has been updated to no longer use the blanket-coverage "always active" tracking option, as the more narrow "first responder" option seems to solve the issue just as well

summary: Make LightmapProbe able to generate multiple probes per node

description.text:
This has several upsides over relying on automatic probe generation alone, or placing individual probe nodes:

- You can choose where to place probes, so that you only place them in areas the player can reach.
- Less manual work is required, as you don't have to place as many nodes.
- Performance is improved in the editor as there are fewer nodes the editor needs to keep track of.
  - Additionally, having fewer nodes in the SceneTree improves runtime performance slightly, since we don't automatically remove LightmapProbe nodes in the exported project yet, even though they're editor-only helpers that have no effect after baking lightmaps.

This works with two new properties:

- **Size (defaults to 1x1x1):** The area in meters covered by the grid of reflection probes.
- **Cell Size (defaults to 2x2x2):** Lower values result in more detailed indirect lighting information for detailed objects.

Cell size is automatically adjusted so that the size is fully covered with probes, without any gaps or irregular spacing.

If cell size is greater than the size on any of the dimensions, only one probe is generated on that dimension, but it will be centered.

The default behavior is to use a size and cell size that generates a single probe in the middle, so that backwards compatibility is preserved.

Note that this does not automatically exclude probes that will be placed inside solid geometry. Something like https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/83420 would be more suited to this approach. (Also, this is likely better implemented in the lightmapper rather than the LightmapProbe node, so that it affects automatically generated probes too.)

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7938.

**Testing project:** [test_lightmap_probe_groups.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19134842/test_lightmap_probe_groups.zip)

## Preview

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3de19391-2e91-45bd-b318-66af83c8dda9)

## TODO

- [ ] Add a visualization for the chosen probe points (similar to the sphere wireframe previously), so you can see what the distribution looks like before baking lightmaps.
  - The gizmo could perhaps be made less intrusive at the same time, e.g. using a small circle/disc billboard icon. It would also be a bit faster to render this way.
- [ ] Write the number of probes the currently selected node will generate at the top of the 3D editor viewport, similar to the VoxelGI VRAM estimation label.
- [ ] Add a hard cap on the number of probes that may be generated per node (or clamp the cell size to a minimum value) to avoid situations where you accidentally add a very large amount of probes, making the bake times nearly infinite.

summary: Intermittent editor freezes after upgrading to 4.4

description.text:
### Tested versions

Didn't happen in 4.3, started happening to me once I upgraded to 4.4.

### System information

MacOS 10.14.6
AMD Radeon R9 M370X 2 GB
Intel Iris Pro 1536 MB

### Issue description

The editor will sometimes "half-freeze", where some things stop working, but others still do. For example, during one of these half-freezes:

* The background highlight that occurs when you hover your mouse over a button stops happening.
* Pressing the "add child node" button doesn't do anything.
* I can still type in the code editor.
* I can still expand and hide dropdown menus in the node inspector.
* But trying to change the value of any of the properties in the inspector doesn't work.
* Clicking and dragging on a node in the scene panel still pops up the little sprite of it to follow the mouse pointer as I drag it elsewhere.
* But releasing it on top of another node doesn't result in the node's position changing in the tree.
* While dragging a node around in the scene panel, the border highlight that normally appears around another node that I've moved the mouse cursor on top of it, or the horizontal line that appears when I'm between nodes, does not appear.

This sometimes solves itself if I wait a few seconds, but usually it does not. What does almost always solve it however is changing the focus to a different, non-Godot window, and then coming back to Godot. (There's only one time that didn't work.)

### Steps to reproduce

It always seems to be triggered by me doing *something*. Usually me adding a child node or changing a property value on an existing node. But I haven't figured out any way to make it happen reliably.

I have tested on a new clean project and it still happens there, so it's not anything specific to the larger project I'm working on.

N/A
